title: Ormosia decemjuga
taxonomy: Ormosia decemjuga is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. This classification places Ormosia decemjuga within the subfamily Faboideae, which comprises over 19,000 species. Ormosia decemjuga is further classified as a member of the tribe Sophoreae. This placement within the tribe indicates a close relationship with other Sophoreae species, such as the katsuratree. The exact classification of Ormosia decemjuga may vary depending on the source or recent taxonomic revisions.
uses: Ormosia decemjuga has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's roots and bark are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to relieve pain and reduce swelling. The medicinal uses of Ormosia decemjuga are largely based on traditional knowledge and have not been extensively scientifically studied. intro: Ormosia decemjuga, also known as the ten-angled bean or ten-angled ormosia, is a flowering plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Leguminosae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ormosia decemjuga include isoflavones, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
